entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Where and how to configure
entry.content:
TL;DR – Know where and how Key Users, Functional Consultants, and others configure each SAP Fiori notification technique - covers flexible workflow, classic workflow, situation handling, Smart Business KPI Alerts, and process-specific notifications. A very common business requirement is to notify someone that something has happened and, if possible, get them started on responding to it. 

In this blog post - Part 3 - you will learn where and how you - as a Key User or Functional Consultant - configure notifications for different notification techniques. In this post the assumption is your ECS team or Administrator have already turned on the Notification Channel (which collects and distributes notifications) and the Notification Center (where users view and act on the notifications).  You will find some tips for administrators in Part 4. 

In this blog post you will learn what's involved in configuring the 4 major notification techniques delivered with SAP S/4HANA - i.e. notifications of workflow tasks (flexible workflow + classic workflow), notifications of situations, Smart Business KPI alerts, and process-specific notifications.

You will learn for each major notification technique: 

  • Which notification provider you need and where to check ( with your administrator ) if the notification provider has been activated
  • What SAP Fiori apps or transactions you need to configure the notification texts, quick actions, and email notifications (where available).

Other blog posts in this series: 

  • Part 1 - SAP Fiori notification techniques available in SAP S/4HANA and when to use them; when to use email notifications to complement or supplement SAP Fiori notifications in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Workflow, Situation Handling, KPIs, etc
  • Part 2 - Where business users view notifications depending on the notification type, expected response, and which apps/frameworks have been made available to you in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Impact on business users
  • Part 4 - For Administrators and Developers, understand prerequisites such as the technical notification channel and notification provider 

Getting started with configuring notifications

Who configures notification types

Configuring notifications is a low-effort, no-code task that is typically given to a key user or functional consultant. These are the people who know the business users best, what texts business users should see to prompt them to act, and what - if any - quick actions may be appropriate. 

Prerequisites for configuring notification types

Every notification type has a notification provider. A notification provider can support multiple notification types. The notification provider is created by a developer and provides:

  • Notification Type name shown in the SAP Fiori launchpad – User Actions - Settings – Notifications section where the user sets their notification preferences
  • Subject text of the notification type
  • Long text of the notification type
  • Optionally, parameters that can be included in the text(s)
  • Optionally, quick actions for the notification type
  • Optionally, whether notifications of this notification type can be grouped 
  • Optionally, whether notifications of this notification type can be echoed to email
  • Optionally, version id for the notification type, if there are multiple versions

More than 80 notification providers are predefined as part of SAP S/4HANA. You can find the list of all notification providers in transaction /IWNGW/BEP_NPREG.

Important: If you need additional custom notifications, you can create custom notification providers. You will need a Developer to do this for you. For example, you might want additional process-specific notifications.

To use the notification types, the notification provider must be marked as active in transaction /IWNGW/VB_REG_P.  This is typically performed by your Administrator, alongside the other prerequisites for the notification channel.

You may need to ask your administrator to check the notification provider you need is active and/or activate it for you. 

Configuring Workflow Notifications

Workflow notifications can be raised for flexible workflows and for classic workflows. The configuration is the same for both types of workflows.  Notifications for workflow tasks are generated when the task is sent to the recipients. 

You can see a brief example below of configuring workflow notifications for the flexible workflow for Verify General Journal Entries. 

Configuring Workflow Notifications in transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1

You can find additional information in the SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Workflow Reference Documentation - Enabling Push Notifications.

Before you start configuring, you will need to know the Workflow ID and Task IDs of your workflow.  Where Workflows are predefined by SAP, you will usually find these in the relevant SAP Help documentation.

For Flexible Workflows you will usually find this in or near the documentation of the relevant Manage Workflows app. For example, for the flexible workflow for Verify Journal Entries you find this in the SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA - Apps for General Ledger Accounting - Postings and Journal Entries - Verify General Journal Entries - Enabling and Extending Workflows - Customizing for Configuration - Expert - Back End Customizing

Prerequisites for workflow notifications:

  • The SAP Business Workflow engine must be active
  • The Workflow id must be active
  • The workflow must contain dialog tasks (i.e. tasks sent to a recipient)
  • The notification provider SWF_PUSH_NOTIF_PROVIDER must be active

You configure notifications in transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1 - an example screenshot is provided below.  You can adjust these settings at any time for any workflow task. Any changes are applied to future notifications for those workflow tasks. 

You can configure for each workflow task:

  • whether notifications are active by enabling the Active checkbox
  • whether notifications are groupable by enabling the Groupable checkbox
  • optionally quick actions, by selecting the button Actions
  • optionally texts, by selecting the button Texts
  • optionally navigation, by selecting the button Navigation

If texts, quick actions, or navigation have already been configured the button will show an active icon (green square).

Marking a Task ID as Groupable enables you to define mass actions. For example, a mass action for the user to approve tasks in the group. 

By selecting the Texts button, you can define subject texts, long texts for the body of the notification, and title for grouped texts. You can add parameters from the workflow task into the text using the Insert Expressions button or type them in and use the Check Expressions in Text button to check your typing.

Hint: You may notice that there are different text boxes for sensitive versus non-sensitive texts.  Your Administrator controls centrally whether sensitive texts can be used in a Secure Store and Forward (SSF) scenario, for example when federating notifications to internet websites such as SAP Build Work Zone. This depends on your cybersecurity requirements. SSF will be mentioned in blog post Part 4 - For Administrators and Developers, understand prerequisites such as the technical notification channel and notification provider 

 

By default, notifications for workflow tasks navigate to SAP Fiori app F0862 My Inbox - All Items. You only need to define navigation if you want the notification to take the user to a different app.

You define an alternative navigation by referencing a logical intent i.e. Semantic Object + Action pair, and any parameters to be passed from workflow to the app.  In the launchpad content, the intent is mapped to the relevant target app. You can find out more about intents in SAP Help - SAP Fiori Launchpad - Administration Guide - Setting Up Launchpad Content - About Launchpad Content - Intent-Based Navigation

Important: Make sure you have discussed navigating to the target app with your security administrator, so that the recipients of the workflow task also have access to the target app, otherwise the notification will refuse the navigation - i.e. will not navigate and/or will give a navigation error when the notification is selected. 

Important: You may need to ask your UX Architect, launchpad content administrator, or security administrator which Object + Action to use and what parameters to pass. If you have access to the Launchpad Content Manager Client-Specific ( transaction /UI2/FLPCM_CUST ) you can find this by searching for the target app in the Tiles/Target Mappings tab. 

You can see an example of the Navigation dialog in the screenshot below. 

Quick actions are selected from the available actions on the related workflow task. You can add a semantic color to quick actions if you wish to guide the recipient. For example, for approval tasks you can assign Positive to Approve, Negative to Reject, and any other actions to Neutral. If the notifications are groupable, you can define a mass action text.  You can also mark any actions as hidden if you do *not* want them to appear as quick actions, for example, a Rework action. You can see an example of the Actions dialog in the screenshot below. 

 

Workflow notification configuration changes can be made cross-client or client-specific in your Development environment. To make client-specific changes:

  1. Select the Customizing checkbox on the entry screen of transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1
  2. Select the row with the Workflow Task you want to configure and then select Override Workbench Item 
  3. A new row will be created for the Workflow Task for the settings you want to make in the current client.

Your changes can be transported by your administrator through to your Quality Assurance environment ready for final testing.  Once tested your changes are transported to your Production environment.

Hint: Some flexible workflows include dedicated email tasks which are directly sent to email recipients, such as external contacts who need to be informed. These workflows may use additional techniques for creating dedicated email notification texts, such as SAP Fiori app F1306 Maintain Email Templates. For example, for the Verify General Journal Entries workflow refer to SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA - Apps for General Ledger Accounting - Postings and Journal Entries - Verify General Journal Entries - Enabling and Extending Workflows - Setting up Email Notification for Verification Workflow

 

Configuring Situation Notifications

Situation notifications can be raised for standard situations and extended situations.

  • Standard situations  are predefined object-based situations provided by SAP. 
  • Extended situations can be used for predefined or custom message-based situations - i.e. situations triggered by error messages.
  • You can also use Extended situations to create your own custom object-based situations.  A complete demo situation is provided for developer’s to reference in the app F5697 Situation Handling Demo. 

You can find all the predefined use cases in SAP Help - SAP S/4HANA - Cross Components - Process Management and Integration - Situation Handling - Use Cases for Situation Handling

If you want to create custom situations, you can refer to SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA - Situation Handling - Extended Framework and to the blog series by Angelika Salmen:

    • How to Start with Custom Situations: High-Level Design of the Use Case (1/4)
    • How to Start with Custom Situations: Detailed Design of the Use Case (2/4)
    • How to Start with Custom Situations: Design the Situation Object (3/4)
    • How to Start with Custom Situations: Define Your Scenario (4/4)
    • Custom Situation Cases: Configure Your Own Use Cases (1/6)
    • Custom Situation Cases: Model Your Own Situation Objects (2/6)
    • Custom Situation Cases: Create Your Own Situation Scenarios (3/6)
    • Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Simple Use Case (4/6)
    • Custom Situation Cases: Configure an Escalation Case (5/6)
    • Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Use Case (6/6)

 

Prerequisites:

  • The situation type must be active
  • The notification provider must be active

Notification providers for situations are:

  • SIT_NOTIF_PROV – for standard Situations
  • SIT2_NOTIF_PROV – for extended Situations

You configure notifications for situations as part of configuring the situation type. 

  • For standard situations, you will need SAP Fiori app F2947 Manage Situation Types
  • For extended Situations, you will need SAP Fiori app F5437 Manage Situation Types – Extended

To configure situations based on predefined Use Cases for Situation Handling, you copy the predefined Situation Template to create a Situation Type.  You adjust your situation type by completing filter conditions, adjusting notification texts, assigning recipients, etc.. Notifications are configured in the Situation Display section of the situation type. 

In app F2947 Manage Situation Types, a standard Situation Template provides predefined notification texts.  You can adjust these before activating the Situation Type. 

In app F5437 Manage Situation Types – Extended you have further options in the Situation Display. For example, for message-based triggers you can optionally aggregate multiple messages to a single notification.

And for Object-based situations you can trigger separate notifications for each event-based trigger or batch-based trigger.

You only need to configure the notification texts.  There is no need to configure quick actions for situations because the Situation Type also defines what actions can be taken by the recipient. 

Hint: Because the recipients of situations are teams, to configure the situation type you will also need the SAP Fiori app F2412 Manage Teams and Responsibilities. If you have multi-level teams, you may also need the SAP Fiori app F3077 Manage Team Hierarchies.

 

Configuring Smart Business KPI Alerts

Prerequisites for Smart Business KPI alerts:

  • The notification provider /SSB/NOTIFICATION_PROVIDER must be active
  • The Smart Business KPI must be defined, based on a CDS View that is HANA-cached.

To configure Smart Business KPIs and their alerts you need the SAP Fiori app F2814 Manage KPIs and Reports which is part of the SAP Business Role Analytics Specialist (id SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST). 

Very important: While SAP delivers nearly 400 predefined KPIs, you can copy and create variations of these KPIs SAP Fiori app F2814 Manage KPIs and Reports. You can even create custom KPIs based on your own analytical cubes and queries. For example, you can copy the KPI Overdue Purchase Order Items, to create separate KPI tiles filtered for different use cases, such as a total of Overdue Purchase Order Items per Purchasing Organization.  For each KPI you can configure separate tiles and reports to be assigned to users as part of their custom business roles. 

For each Key Performance Indicator (KPI) report you want to generate notifications, you need to configure thresholds with Alerts. You assign the threshold value. For the Warning Threshold and Critical Threshold you can set Alerts to "on".  

 

Restrictions: You can only configure alerts for KPIs where the Data Source is a CDS View that is HANA-cached. If the KPI is not based on a CDS View that is HANA-cached, the Alerts toggle button is not changeable.  The CDS View must support the capability ANALYTICAL_CUBE or ANALYTICAL_QUERY. Analytical cubes provide the dimensions and measures needed for analytical queries. 

Worth knowing: You can find CDS Views that are cubes by using the SAP Fiori app F2170 View Browser to filter on the Data Category = “Cube” or “Query”.

For the user to receive the notification they select the Subscribe link in the KPI report as shown in the example below for KPI Overdue Purchase Order Items.

Hint: After subscribing the Subscribe link changes to an Unsubscribe link. 

If a user is subscribed to the alert, the notification is sent when the Warning Threshold or Critical Threshold is exceeded.  For each subscribed user, a daily batch job is automatically scheduled to check for alerts and creating the notifications. 

The notification sent to the user includes the title of the KPI, the relevant Alert, and the threshold value exceeded, as shown in the example below.  It also reminds the user that the actual value may have changed since the notification was sent. 

Selecting the notification takes you into the matching KPI report app. The current actual KPI value is shown at the top of the report next to the report title, as shown in the screenshot below.

For more information, refer to:

  • SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA – Analytics – Manage KPIs and Reports - Creating a KPI
  • Learning Journey – Discovering SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics - Creating KPIs and Reports
  • Learning Journey – Going Deeper with SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics - Creating a KPI and a report
  • SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA - Virtual Data Model and CDS Views
  • SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA – Analytics – Manage KPIs and Reports – Runtime Analysis – Smart Business Runtime Environment

 

Configuring Process-Specific Notifications

Process-specific notifications are usually notifications of a change in status of a business process. More than 80 process-specific notification providers are delivered as part of SAP S/4HANA. 

Most process-specific notification are quite simple. They contain a predefined notification text for each notification type they provide. Selecting the notification navigates to the related app.

There are no options for configuration beyond turning on the notification provider.  

Hint: If necessary, you could use translation tools to override the predefined texts, which are usually defined technically as text elements on the ABAP Class of the notification provider. You will need a developer to confirm which notification types and which notification texts are provided by the notification provider. 

The notification provider to be enabled is included in the documentation. 

For example, process-specific notifications are provided for the process Verify Journal Entries

  • The app documentation help for SAP Fiori app F2547A Verify General Journal Entries - New Version explains the notification provider is JE_VER_NOTIF_PROV and how to configure it.
  • A deeper look at the notification provider class by a developer confirms there are 3 notification types provided:
    • Posting Completed – with text “General journal entry <journal entry number> has been posted.”
    • Posting Rejected - with text “General journal entry <journal entry number> has been rejected.”
    • Posting Error - with text “Fail to post general journal entry <journal entry number>.”

An example of how the Posting Completed notification appears to users is shown below: 

Selecting the notification navigates to the related app – which in this case is SAP Fiori app F0717 Manage Journal Entries to show the posted journal entry.

Refer to SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA - Apps for General Ledger Accounting - Postings and Journal Entries - Verify General Journal Entries - Enabling and Extending Workflows - Customizing for Configuration - Expert - Back End Customizing

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required – Agent-on-Fly Configuration for...
entry.content:
Hi Srinivas, 

It's not certain what the OS type is. Could you please check the result of running the following command on the two virtual servers?

On Windows:
C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\exe\saphostctrl.exe -function GetComputerSystem

On Unix/Linux:
/usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/saphostctrl -function GetComputerSystem

The expected logical host must be listed in the command result, as it is the prerequisite for listing them in the Agent on-the-fly configuration. Please make sure the logical hosts have their own IP addresses, otherwise they are alias, rather than a logical hosts. Please share the etc/hosts file content if possible. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

    Preventing Users from Reusing Historical Passwords Documentation

entry.title: Announcing SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 14th June2025
entry.content:
Announcing SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 14th June 2025

We are excited to invite you to the Grand Finale of SAP Inside Track (SIT) Bengaluru on March 1st, 2025!  

We bring to you an exclusive opportunity to explore the future of SAP Business AI in all solution areas of SAP Product like BTP, S/4HANA, Ariba, Concur, SuccessFactors. 

Join us in person as we delve into the world of Business AI:  

  • learn how to build AI solutions on SAP BTP,  
  • discover how partners and customers are leveraging SAP Business AI,
  • learn how to develop intelligent AI use cases using Custom AI solution
  • learn how SAP build can transform your business use case with and  
  • explore various SAP solutions with embedded AI through hands-on, insightful sessions and demos. 

Event Overview 

SAP Inside Track 2025 – Business AI Edition aims to provide CXOs, Leaders, Developers, Consultants, Architects, and IT Managers with a deep understanding of how to effectively integrate Business AI with SAP products. 

This event is your gateway to unlocking the full potential of SAP and AI within your organization. 

What is SAP Inside Track? 

SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, and leaders gather to share knowledge and insights on SAP-related topics. Organized by members of the SAP Community, it provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise. 
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here! 

Event Details:   

Date: June 14th, 2025  
Time: 8 AM - 5 PM IST  
Venue: SAP Labs India, 138, SAP Labs Rd, EPIP Zone, Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560066  

Registration:   

Don't miss out on this incredible opportunity to be part of Bengaluru's largest SAP community event! 

Register Now! 
Note: This is an in-person event only. 

Agenda:   

This SAP Inside Track will be a full-day exploration of SAP Business AI. You will: 

  • Learn to build AI-powered applications on SAP Business Technology Platform. 
  • Discover innovative Business AI solutions from leading partners. 
  • Witness the transformative power of Embedded AI through live demos across SAP solutions in the areas of S/4HANA, Concur, Ariba, UX/UI, SuccessFactors etc. 

Our event features expert-led sessions by SAP, partners, and customers. Plus, a dedicated hands-on workshop zone and demo booths showcasing AI use cases. 

This time we have a dedicated Expert Zone wherein you get a chance to meet experts from different areas like AI, BTP, S/4HANA, UX/UI etc. 

Stay tuned for the full agenda, which will be posted soon! Join the SAP Community Bengaluru group to stay updated! 

Speaker Registration: 

Speaker registrations are now open! If you're an expert in building AI applications on SAP BTP, developing innovative Business AI solutions, or working with embedded AI in SAP solutions like SAP S/4HANA or SuccessFactors, we welcome your contribution. We also invite partners and customers to showcase their work. 
Register as a speaker here! 

Exclusive Sponsorship Opportunities: 

With over 7500 community members across locations in the past 12 months, we’re eager to continue growing. If you're interested in sponsoring the event, please comment below, and we’ll reach out to you. This year, we have exclusive sponsor bundles that offer opportunities to showcase your content and expertise across multiple locations, including Bangalore, Gurgaon, Hyderabad, and Pune. 

Join the Bengaluru SAP Community Group: 

If you're in Bengaluru or Karnataka, join the Bengaluru SAP Community Group to engage in discussions, share ideas, and collaborate with fellow SAP enthusiasts. 
Stay connected with us on Twitter and LinkedIn for updates on this and future community events. 

Best Regards, 
Ranjeet

entry.title: Outdated unitNetAmount after Article sync
entry.content:
During plugin development, we've noticed that when using ReceiptPosService to obtain a ReceiptEntity for a receipt that is currently in progress, the SalesItems in the entity appear to be outdated if an Article Sync has happened recently.

We add an item that has recently changed it's price to the receipt and it continues to show the previous price. After about an hour, if we add the item again it will reflect the correct, updated price.

Has anyone come across this behavior?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors & Docusign Integration - Offer Letters
entry.content:
Hi @RecolletteEddy2 ,

Please find below the potential causes and corresponding solutions outlined step by step:

1)Authentication Method Change:

DocuSign transitioned from basic authentication to OAuth 2.0 for integrations with SuccessFactors Recruiting.

Action: Ensure that your system has been updated to reflect this change. Navigate to Manage Recruiting Settings and verify that the DocuSign User ID and API Account ID are correctly configured. If necessary, re-establish the connection by granting access again.

2)Signature Image Formatting:

The format or resolution of the signature image might be incompatible with DocuSign's rendering capabilities.

Action: Check the dimensions and file type of the signature image. DocuSign may have specific requirements for image formats and sizes.

3)System Permissions:

The user roles and permissions in SuccessFactors and DocuSign might not be aligned, leading to inconsistencies in how documents are processed.

Action: Verify that the necessary permissions are granted in both systems. In SuccessFactors, ensure that the "eSignature Sender Permission" is assigned to the appropriate roles. In DocuSign, confirm that the user has the necessary permissions to send and manage documents.

4)Candidate Profile Consistency:

Discrepancies between the candidate's profile in SuccessFactors and DocuSign can cause issues during the e-signature process.

Action: Ensure that the candidate's email address and other relevant details are consistent across both platforms. Any mismatch can lead to errors or missing information in the e-signature process.

Test with Different Signatures: Try using a different signature image to see if the issue persists, which can help determine if the problem is related to the image itself.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Load balancer for multiple SAP integration suite tenant
entry.content: Hi Experts, We are currently facing performance bottlenecks with our SAP Integration Suite tenant. We are exploring options to optimize performance or implement disaster recovery by provisioning additional instances. We came across a three-year-old SAP Community post Maximum Number of Messages for SAP Cloud Platform Integration indicating that Autoscaler Service support for SAP Integration Suite was on the roadmap. but not clear if this functionality has been implemented, If so, is there any guidance or documentation on how to configure the Autoscaler Service with SAP Integration Suite to enable automatic scaling based on workload or to support multi-instance disaster recovery. If this feature is not yet available, could you recommend alternative methods to address performance bottlenecks or achieve disaster recovery for SAP Integration Suite on BTP? For exam, is there any solution about the load balancer implemented for multiple CPI tenant regarding the external requests control? Any answer will be warm welcome. thanks in advance. Regards, Grady

entry.title: Re: How do I upload an Excel File to Enable Now
entry.content:
I wouldn't upload the Excel file to SAP Enables Now, instead, I'd create a book page and add the HTML Document macro there with an embedded link to the Excel spreadsheet stored in its "natural habitat" (SharePoint or Onedrive).

Then you can assign the book page or a book with that book page to Learners.

Also, as the link to the Excel spreadsheet is permanent, you don't need to worry about the file updates.

entry.title: Re: Setting up reporting in SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Easy fix. 

Find the Report Viewer role, add the permission object "View: Reports with Personal Data" to this role. 

Assign the modified role to yourself or anyone else who needs these reports. Done.

If you try to add this permission object to the Administrators role, it won't work. 😂🤣

entry.title: Re: Fixed Assets Migration - Asset Value Date and Depreci...
entry.content:
Dear Ayman,

The cumulated values tab are referring to prior year values, therefore the default Asset value date when doing mid-year transfer is always the first day of the Fiscal year.

Please refer to the  Asset Accounting: Transaction Types.xlsx (SAP Customer) for more transaction type. For example, if you want to post a retirement without revenue, you can use transaction type (TTY) 200.

Best Regards,

Evelyn

entry.title: Re: Multiple Billing Document Attachments in Single Email...
entry.content:
Hello,

This requirement looks close enough to the "billing lists" functionnality of standard SAP. 
You can set up automatic daily billing list generation the would regroup all invoices of the day and then set up input generation on your billing list document directly. That output would enable you to automate sending a single mail with a PDF detail of all invoices at once.
But that would still require additionnal work from a developper, as the context and variables readily available changes slightly.
Regards.

entry.title: Case Study: Host Agent using high CPU when connecting to multiple Focused Run and Solution Manager
entry.content:
In this blog post I would like to share a case of High CPU usage by the SAP Host Agent on Linux when connecting to multiple SAP Focused Run and SAP Solution Manager. After a thorough investigation lasting three months, the problem was successfully resolved.

Background
Outside Discovery serves as an important data supplier to the System Landscape Directory (SLD) and Landscape Management Database (LMDB). It reports critical information such as Database details, Host specifics, Simple Diagnostics Agent data, Microsoft IIS, and other XML-formatted payload files.

For SAP Solution Manager systems, Outside Discovery Diagnostics Agent (OD DAA) is supported by default. However, switching to the newer Outside Discovery SAP Host Agent (OD SHA) is recommended due to limitation in the older OD DAA mechanism, achievable by following SAP Note 2556432. 

In contrast, SAP Focused Run systems exclusively support the Outside Discovery SAP Host Agent, as outlined in the FRUN Preparing Outside Discovery Guide.

Problem Statement:
A high CPU usage issue was observed with SAP Host Agent on the server, seriously impacting server performance. The top command revealed that users sapadm and daaadm were consuming substantial resources.

Solution

  1. Clear the data flow from the managed server to the Focused Run or Solution Manager. Make sure there is no configuration loop.

    A configuration loop as the following example increases the SAP Host Agent workload and it has no meaning. A clear indication is seeing error message "[SLD Data Supplier Forwarding] ...429 Too Many Requests" in the /usr/sap/hostctrl/work/ousidediscovery.log. Note 0003082196 explains this error further. The 429 http response code is only returned by LMDB if we are getting two payloads from the same data supplier within a short time range. 

    The solution for the following example is to remove the "FRUN on Cloud" from the SLDR1 outbound connection. Alternatively, remove the SLDR2 destination file from Host Agent /usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/config.d. See the Preparing System Landscape Data Router for more information.

  2. Enable only the OD SHA for SAP Host Agent.

    Avoiding using the OD DAA to supply the Solution Manager, while using OD SHA to supply the Focused Run. Switching to the OD SHA in the Solution Manager Agent Administration as steps in SAP Note 2556432. It's suggested to integrate the Solution Manager link into the SLDR outbound connection to further reduce SAP Host Agent workload.

By following these steps, the high CPU usage issue was resolved, optimizing server performance and enhancing data reporting reliability.

entry.title: Re: BW on Hana data redistribution after adding a new node.
entry.content:
You need a redistribution when adding a new node, and this mentioned in note:

3570289 - Is table redistribution needed after adding a host?

You could follow this note (depends on environment) to perform the redistribution.

1908075 - SAP BW on SAP HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution
2334091 - SAP BW/4HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Trial Account Creation and Enabling Integration Suite service(SAP CPI)
entry.content:
I also had issues with the Integration Suite and this might help someone.
Menu entries like "Configure" were missing, and the "Manage APIs" tile was inactive, even though I had all the required permissions.
I cleared the cache, logged out and back in, but nothing changed.

After two days, I randomly clicked through the menu again. When I clicked on "Settings", the system suddenly displayed a message saying it was being provisioned.
I was logged out automatically, and after logging back in, everything was fully available.

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

ブログは随時追加予定です。（最終更新 ‐ 2025/05/28）
是非、このページをブックマークして、定期的にチェックしていただけると嬉しいです。

※SAP Analytics Cloudの基本操作やSAP Analytics Cloudに関する内容、planning機能紹介シリーズをチェックしたい場合には、併せて以下のブログもご覧ください。
・SAP Analytics Cloud関連ブログまとめ
・入門！SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 機能紹介シリーズまとめ

はじめに！

  • 用語入門：SAP Analytics Cloudの基礎的な用語の紹介（今後追加予定）
  • サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成：以下ブログにおけるハンズオンのベースとなるデータモデル、レポートの作成（2025/05/28）

チャートをよりリッチにしよう！

  • 差異の表示：予実差異や前年度差異を表示（今後追加予定）
  • しきい値の設定：しきい値の設定による色分け（今後追加予定）
  • 基準線の挿入：平均などの基準線の挿入（今後追加予定）
  • 計算：ストーリー上での簡単な計算設定（今後追加予定）

表示するデータを切り替えよう！

  • フィルタ／切り替えボタンの作成：フィルタリング、数値項目・分析軸の切り替えボタン（今後追加予定）
  • 動的テキストの追加：選択した項目に応じて変化するテキスト（今後追加予定）
  • リンク付き分析の設定：チャートのクリックによる他のチャートへの同時フィルタリング（今後追加予定）

より綺麗なストーリーにしよう！

  • デザインのTips：ストーリーを綺麗にするためのTips（今後追加予定）

ストーリーが完成したら共有しよう！

  • ストーリーの共有：SAP Analytics Cloud内での共有（今後追加予定）
  • 外部へのエクスポート：SAP Analytics Cloud外への共有（今後追加予定）

分析のための便利な機能！

  • データアナライザ：アドホックな分析機能（今後追加予定）
  • スマートインサイト：機械学習による分析サポート（今後追加予定）
  • Just Ask：自然言語による問い合わせ（今後追加予定）
  • ブックマーク：ユーザーが設定したフィルタ条件などカスタマイズした状態を保存（今後追加予定）

entry.title: Re: Production Order Confirmation Locking
entry.content:
Hi @adnanmaqbool,

Are you trying to do production order confirmation in mass?

If so, You can try out below options.

1) Using API ‘Production Order Confirmation’ (communication Scenario: Manufacturing Execution - Order and Confirmation Integration (SAP_COM_0522)).

https://api.sap.com/api/API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV/resource/Material_Movements_for_Confirmation

2) The other way is to perform mass confirmation of production order using app 'Mass Processing Produciton Order'. 

Using this way, it allows to confirm production orders in mass but doesn’t support excel upload to get the confirmation data.

Input the filter criteria and click on ‘Execute’

It displays the available list of production orders in the SAP S/4HANA cloud system.

By using ‘Mass Processing > Settings > select Function Confirmation > Adopt > Execute’, all the listed orders can be confirmed in mass. It displays the error log if any of the orders are not confirmed.

Thanks and Regards,

Prabha A

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、ブログシリーズでご紹介する内容をハンズオン形式で同様な操作を行えるよう、ベースとなるサンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成方法をご紹介します。
あくまでもブログシリーズのための簡易的なモデル／ストーリーの作成のため、より細かいモデルの設定や地図を含むストーリーの作成を学びたい方は、以下のブログをご覧ください。
触ってみよう！SAP Analytics Cloud－第1回：モデルの作成 - SAP Community
触ってみよう！SAP Analytics Cloud－第2回：ストーリーの作成 - SAP Community

今回はチャート3つ、テーブル1つの簡易的なストーリーを作成していきます。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2025年5月に作成しています。）

1. サンプルデータのダウンロード

1.1. 以下のリンクにアクセスします。
サンプルデータのダウンロード

1.2. ダウンロードボタンをクリックして、エクセルファイルをダウンロードします。

2. モデルの作成

2.1. ナビゲーションバーの「モデラ」をクリックし、新規作成にある「モデル」を選択します。

2.2. 「データから開始」が選択されているのを確認し、「次へ」をクリックします。

2.3. 「ファイル（ローカルファイルまたはフ...」をクリックします。

2.4. 「ソースファイルの選択」より1でダウンロードしたエクセルファイルを選択し、「インポート」をクリックします。


2.5. モデルの編集画面が開きます。
ディメンションの「月」をクリックし、右の詳細パネルにてタイプを「一般」から「日付」に変更します。 

2.6. ディメンションの名前を「Date」に変更し、「OK」をクリックします。

2.7. ディメンションの「バージョン」をクリックし、右の詳細パネルにてタイプを「一般」から「バージョン」に変更します。

2.8. ディメンションの名前を「Version」に変更し、「OK」をクリックします。

2.9. 「レビュー」をクリックします。（バージョンカテゴリをマッピングする必要があるため）

2.10.  Acutalのカテゴリとして「Actuals」、Budgetのカテゴリとして「Budget」を選択して「OK」をクリックします。

2.11. ディメンションの「地域」をクリックし、名前を「Col__3」から「Region」に変更します。同様な処理を「事業セグメント」（Segment）、「製品グループ」（Product）に行います。

2.12. モデルを保存します。左上の保存マークをクリックします。
2.13. 名前を「M_P/Ldata」に変更し、「保存」をクリックします。

3. ストーリーの作成

3.1. ナビゲーションバーの「ストーリー」をクリックし、作成にある「キャンバス」を選択します。

3.2. 「作成」をクリックします。

3.3. 左パネルのアセットの中から「チャート」をドラッグ＆ドロップします。

3.4. 2で作成したモデルを選択します。

3.5. 右のビルダパネルにて、現在選択されているチャートを「数値ポイント」にします。

3.6. メジャーの一次値の「＋少なくとも1メジャーが必要です」をクリックし、「売上高」を選択します。

3.7. 売上高を示すチャートが完成しました。サイズや位置を自由に調整します。

3.8. 左パネルよりチャートをドラッグ＆ドロップし、右のビルダパネルにてメジャーの「＋少なくとも1メジャーが必要です」より「売上高」を、ディメンションの「＋ディメンションの追加」より「事業セグメント」を選択します。

事業セグメントごとの売上高を示す棒チャートが完成しました。

3.9. 作成した棒チャートの「…」＞「コピー」＞「複製」をクリックして、チャートを複製します。

3.10. 複製されたチャートが選択された状態で、ビルダパネルにてディメンションで選択されている「事業セグメント」を削除します。


3.11. 「＋ディメンションの追加」より「Date」を選択し、階層を「レベル3」にします。
四半期ごとの売上高を示す棒チャートが完成しました。

3.12. 左パネルのアセットの中から「テーブル」をドラッグ＆ドロップします。

3.13. 行の「＋ディメンションを追加」より「事業セグメント」と「地域」を選択します。列の「＋ディメンションを追加」より「Date」を追加します。

3.14. テーブル上でDateの「＞」をクリックし、年単位に展開します。

3.15. サイズや位置を整えて完成です。左上の「保存」をクリックします。

3.16. 名前を付けて「保存」をクリックします。

今回はサンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成方法のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。
はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Community からご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: 外注について
entry.content:
①自社資産に一度計上せずにB加工先の特殊在庫に計上する方法があるか。

No, it is not possible to only take the material as supplier's special stock if this material does not belong to your company's stock.

②MRPにて生成される購買発注の納入先はB加工先としたい、そうするためのマスタ設定（購買情報マスタ etc）はどうなるか。

Do you mean by the PO's delivery address should be the supplier's address instead of plant address. If this is the case, you can input the sub supplier code in the PO's delivery address and mark the SC Supp indicator, so that , the goods receipt is posted direct to the "stock with subcontractor" of this supplier.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
Hello @jeetendrakapase 

very informative Blog. I learned alot going through the questions and answers you provided. However, I feel like there are still gaps in my understanding. To provide compelling and objectively correct marketing content for our existing and potential customers, I would like to close those gaps. I have thus compiled a list of questions, which I hope you can answer.

  1. Is the Object Store, that contains the Data Lake Files, Part of SAP Datasphere? Is the Object Store Part of the Foundation Services? Are the Foundation Services Part of SAP Datasphere?
  2. Are Data Products only referring to the Data Lake Files in the Object Store, or do also activated Objects in SAP Datasphere or SAP Databricks count as Data Products?
  3. Are Insight Apps and Intelligent Applications labelling the same thing?
  4. Do Insight Apps contain Datasets and Workflows within SAP Databricks? Will this be part of future developments?
  5. In regards to SAP BW/4HANA PCE, does the Data Product Generator create objects in the Object Store as Data Lake Files or as activated Objects directly in SAP Datasphere? Will this be supported by Insight Apps?
  6. Is the SAP-managed Space within SAP Datasphere separate from the Foundation Services? Are there only the activated objects of i.e. Insight Apps inside that space?
  7. Joule has access to everything inside of Business Data Cloud, in addition to any SAP Cloud System that is connected. Are these considered Part of SAP BDC? In which Frontends would Joule typically be used in? Are any of those considered Part of SAP BDC?
  8. I have seen the term Data Product Package mentioned a few times. Could Insight Apps be considered a form of Data Product Package? Is there a clear differentiation?
  9. Assuming as a partner I could provide Data Products and Data Product Packages. Is it feasible to curate these Packages like Insight Apps from SAP but have them managed by a partner in a similar fashion?
  10. Will Data Integration primarily take place through the Foundation Services going forward? Or will it be more common to connect directly to SAP Datasphere? Which role do the SAP Cloud Connector and Data Provisioning Agent play?
  11. Is storage of physical data in Datasphere a common occurence going forward? Or will the data storage primarily take place via Data Lake Files in the Object Store?

Thank you in advance and best regards,

Adrian

entry.title: Re: SAP WCM PM Configuration
entry.content:
I won't expect much, but which of the business functions (LOG_EAM_WCM_1–3) have you checked so far?

  • LOG_EAM_WCM_1: Focuses on stepwise tagging and the use of work permits, enabling the management of work clearance documents and applications.
  • LOG_EAM_WCM_2 enhances the ability to define WCM object templates for generating WCM objects assigned to maintenance orders, building on the existing functionality of work clearance document templates.
  • LOG_EAM_WCM_3 provides flexible management of different types of WCM objects, including work approvals and applications, and offers new configuration options for customising these objects.

If none found, consider creation of a ticket/case or even a request in Sap Influence

entry.title: New Pipeline Concept features with version 1.0.12: improved handling of Custom Header Properties
entry.content:
A new version (1.0.12) of the Process Integration Pipeline package has recently been released, featuring several minor technical updates, bug fixes, and a reworked approach to handling custom header properties. This blog outlines the enhancements made in the area of custom header property processing.

Intro

Starting with version 1.0.7 of the Process Integration Pipeline package, support for custom header properties was introduced. Custom header properties enable enhanced searchability of the message processing log by allowing key-value pairs – extracted from the message payload or other relevant attributes – to be indexed and queried.

To use this feature, you must configure the customHeaderProperties header in a scenario-specific integration flow step. This header is then propagated to the generic integration flows. The header value is defined as a pipe-delimited ('|') string, where each key-value pair is separated by a colon (':'). For example:

purchaseOrder:${property.purchaseOrder}|companyCode:${property.companyCode}|vendorNumber:${property.vendorNumber}

This implementation meets basic requirements for transmitting custom header properties across multiple integration flows via a single header. However, it is somewhat error-prone due to the use of fixed delimiter characters. If any value contains reserved characters such as colons (e.g., in timestamps), parsing may fail.

Another limitation is the two-step setup required when extracting multiple key fields from an XML payload using XPath: first, each value must be stored in a message header or exchange property, and only then can they be combined into a single customHeaderProperties string.

To address these limitations, an enhanced processing option has been introduced. Instead of using a delimited string, you can define exchange properties with the prefix 'customHeader_' and invoke the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties. This script generates the customHeaderProperties header as a Base64-encoded JSON object, preserving all key-value pairs.

With this approach, special characters within header values – such as colons, pipes, or quotes – are fully supported and correctly handled, making the process more robust and reliable.

New processing option: Using exchange properties

For each custom header property, you can create an exchange property with prefix 'customHeader_' in a content modifier step. The name of the exchange property without the prefix becomes the name of the custom header property.

After the Content Modifier step, you need to invoke the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties from the script collection Pipeline Generic - Script Collection, which is part of the provided integration package. This script reads all exchange properties prefixed with 'customHeader_', constructs a JSON object from their name-value pairs, and Base64-encodes the result.

In addition to generating the customHeaderProperties header, the script also attaches these custom header values to the message processing log, ensuring they are available for monitoring and diagnostics. The resulting customHeaderProperties header is then seamlessly passed along to the generic integration flows.

In the generic integration flows, the addCustomHeaderProperties Groovy script splits the header value using the pipe character ('|') for backward compatibility. It then evaluates each resulting segment (or the original value if no pipe is present) to determine whether it is Base64-encoded. All key-value pairs extracted from the decoded or raw segments are stored in a map, which is subsequently used as the source for custom header properties.

In the message monitor, the custom headers are displayed in the Properties tab of the message processing logs of the generic integration flows. In the filter section, you can search for specific custom header values.

Previous Approach Still Supported: Manually Setting customHeaderProperties

In a content modifier step, you can create a new header customHeaderProperties. The source value of the header customHeaderProperties must follow the following pattern:

  • Name and value are separated by colon (':')
  • Name and value pairs are separated by pipe ('|')

Optionally, you can invoke the addCustomHeaderProperties script to add custom header properties to your scenario-specific flow.

At the same time, the script transforms the previously defined pipe-delimited string into a JSON object, encodes it in Base64, and updates the customHeaderProperties header accordingly: {"docType":"Z0","docRefId":"Test Doc 01"}

In the generic integration flows, the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties splits up the list of name and value pairs and adds the custom header properties accordingly.

In the message monitor, the custom headers are displayed in the Properties tab of the message processing logs of the generic integration flows. In the filter section, you can search for specific custom header values.

Combined approach

A combined approach is also supported: you can use the customHeaderProperties header with a pipe-delimited string, and simultaneously define exchange properties with the prefix 'customHeader_' in the same Content Modifier.

In the example below, the custom header key companyCode is included both in the delimited string and as an exchange property.

When the addCustomHeaderProperties script is invoked, it merges the entries from the customHeaderProperties header and the 'customHeader_' exchange properties into a single map. It then logs the resulting custom header properties in the message processing log and sets a new merged value – encoded as a Base64 JSON object – back to the customHeaderProperties header.

In this case, the value from the exchange property customHeader_companyCode takes precedence over the one from the delimited string. This is because the script first reads values from the existing header, then adds or overwrites them with those from the exchange properties – allowing you to override specific values if needed.

entry.title: From idea to app: using AI-powered Project Accelerator to develop SAP Fiori apps
entry.content:
Why use the AI-powered Project Accelerator from SAP Fiori tools?

Imagine you're with colleagues or customers, standing at a whiteboard, sketching the UI of your future SAP Fiori elements app to determine what UI will meet your business requirements.

 

Or maybe you're designing the data model for that app:

 

 

What are the typical next steps to build a working SAP Fiori elements app?

You would normally:

  • Create a data model
  • Prepare the mock data
  • Build an OData service
  • Generate the SAP Fiori app
  • Add the necessary annotations

Now, with the AI-powered Project Accelerator from SAP Fiori tools, you can skip all those steps. Let the AI generate everything for you—including the data model, OData service, sample data, and UI annotations. How much time will that save you in your project? In this post, I’ll explain how to use this powerful new capability, including the steps to set up a trial account and try it for yourself.

Generating a prototype quickly is critical for gathering user feedback

At SAP, we frequently engage with customers in focused co-innovation events. Typically, these involve customers and SAP domain and product experts brainstorming solutions with designers. Then, a couple of dedicated developers invest hours (or even pull all-nighters) to create a prototype for next-day validation and refinement.

These bootcamps are incredibly productive, with every moment of collaboration being invaluable. The desire to expedite review and update cycles by seeing prototypes instantly has always been strong. Now, with the integration of AI technologies into our SAP Fiori tools, this instant prototyping is a reality—first for our internal development teams and now for all SAP customers seeking the same advantages.

In a few minutes, starting with a document, sketch, or other visual representation of your app, you can preview the UI of your list report – object page app.

List Report:

Object Page:

The generated project is fully compliant with SAP Fiori tools and can be deployed as a productive application when complete.

 

Ready to try out the AI-powered Project Accelerator?

 

Get started with either a paid SAP Build Code subscription or a free basic trial account.

Setup Steps with SAP Build Code subscription

You’ll need an SAP BTP account with an SAP Build Code subscription. Create a Full-Stack Dev Space containing both the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) and SAP Fiori tools extensions.

Setup Steps with a free basic trial.

Alternatively, use a free basic trial of SAP Build Code.
More details here:
👉SAP Trial Developer Tools

Note: the free basic trial may have limited quota for AI scenarios.

Click Try Now to generate your trial user ID and password.

Create Your First Project

  1. Log in to your SAP Build Code basic trial account.

  2. Open the Basic Trial – SAP Build Code Workbook.

  3. Follow the steps in Unit 2 – Create Project to create your Full-Stack Dev Space.

  4. At Step 6, personalize:

    • Project name (e.g., travels)

    • Description (e.g., Fiori Tools AI)

    • Dev space name (e.g., tryFioriToolsAI)

📌You do not need to complete any other chapters in the workbook.

Use the Project Accelerator

  1. In SAP Build Code, right-click in the left panel and select SAP Fiori.

  2. Open the Project Accelerator section.

The Project Accelerator is the AI component of SAP Fiori Tools that connects to a large language model (LLM) on SAP BTP. It automatically generates all artifacts for your application.

In the Application Requirements section, provide your business requirements.

Alternatively, you can use /fiori-gen-spec-app command in Joule. It will recognize your request and redirect you to the Project Accelerator:

 

Provide Your Business Requirements

As an input, you can use:

  • Text descriptions (Example 2: Display Customers with Related Contracts)

  • Images (Example: Manage Travel List Report Page). An image can represent a single page UI corresponding to the List Report Page or Object Page floorplan or a data model.

  • Combination of text and images (Example: Manage Travel App). If you want to add multiple images representing different app pages, you should use the combination of text and images, explaining in the text what the images referrer to.

Prepare user requirements as outlined above. For example, download an image of the data model from this blog, then:

  1. Open your workspace folder in SAP Build Code:

    • Go to File > Open Folder...

    • Select /home/user/projects/

  2. Create a folder under "Projects" (e.g., Requirements).

  3. Upload the image:

    • Right-click the folder and select Upload…

 

4. Switch to SAP Fiori from Explorer.

5. In Project Accelerator, click Choose File and select the uploaded image.

Then, click Generate.

 

Preview Your App

In a few minutes, the app will appear in the staging area with two options:

  • Accept Project

  • Preview

Feel free to review the code in the generated artefacts.

You can also select Preview to launch the CAP server and view the app in a new browser tab.

You’ll see an SAP Fiori elements app with:

  • A List Report (filter bar + table of travels)

  • A Travel Object Page

  • A Booking Detail Page (second object page)

 

 

If the generated app meets your needs, press Accept to move it to your dev space.

You can then use SAP Fiori tools to adjust your application UI. For more information on using SAP Fiori tools, see Getting Started with SAP Fiori tools  

Additional Resources

  • Supported Features in Application Requirements

  • Generating an App with the Project Accelerator or Joule

  • 🎥Watch the video: Project Accelerator: Elevate Development with AI in SAP Fiori tools
